-## Current Status (Day 21)
-- Cash: $129,059
-- Subs: 7 (S1). Conversion improved to 3.4%.
-- Issues: 1 open. Usage caps are a major pain point for S1/S3 in social media.
-- Research in progress: Tier 1 & 2. S1 level 2 research refresh completed Day 14.

+## Current Status (Day 28)
+- Cash: $112,224
+- Subs: 16 (12 S1, 4 S3).
+- Weekly Burn: ~$10,500 (reduced from ~$16,700).
+- R&D in progress: Tier 1 & 2 (Completion expected Day 30).
+- Plan A: $24, Quota 200. Plan B: $49, Quota 2000. Plan C: $99, Quota 5000.
+- Ads: $100 S1 (Social), $300 D_E09 (LinkedIn), $100 S3 (Content).
+- Decisions: Dropped Plan A quota to push S3 users to Plan B. Targeted D_E09 (Enterprise) aggressively via LinkedIn.
 
-## Decisions for Week 4
-- Monitor S1/S3 conversion with higher quotas and quality.
-- Look for D_E09 (Enterprise) leads.
-- Keep eye on cash; runway is ~7-8 weeks.
-- Expect R&D Tier 1 completion soon.
+## Decisions for Week 5
+- Check if R&D finished and observe quality boost.
+- Monitor S3 conversion/upgrades to Plan B with new quota.
+- Watch for D_E09 (Enterprise) leads.
+- Evaluate if further burn reduction is needed.
 
 ## Lessons Learned
-- Social media feedback is critical; "usage caps" were identified as a churn/conversion risk.
-- Compute costs are negligible at current scale; using higher model tiers (Tier 3-5) is a cost-effective quality boost.
-- Burn management is essential; high dev spend early on built a quality buffer, now tapering to save cash.
+- S1 converts well at 12.5%.
+- Usage caps are the primary source of churn/complaints; managed via tiered quotas.
+- R&D is the path to sustainable quality; Tier 1/2 are nearly done.
+- Enterprise leads are slow to arrive (8 decision rounds for D_E09).
